items: If the cap is removed when the water temperature is high, the coolant is under pressure and may spurt out, which is dangerous. After the water temperature has dropped, wrap the cap with a piece of cloth and open it gently.

blocks.text: Remove the radiator cap. Fill the upper tank to the top with cooling water. Install the radiator cap tester adapter (ACKER-GP0-0110) and tester in the radiator cap opening. Pump up to the normal pressure value and inspect for leaks. Normal pressure: 0.5 to 1.0 MPa 94-122kPa (0.96－1.24kgf/cm 2 ) Radiator cap tester Remove the adapter and tester, and install the radiator cap.

items.text: If the pressure drops in a short time, the tester is poorly installed or leaking water.
If the pressure drops in a short time, the tester is poorly installed or leaking water.

If oil is mixed in, engine seals are defective.

Inspect for mixing of coolant and transmission fluid.
Inspect for mixing of coolant and transmission fluid.
If transmission fluid is mixed with transmission fluid, CVT warmer is defective.
If transmission fluid is mixed with transmission fluid, CVT warmer is defective.
